cards.back: An unsupervised learning algorithm used for partitioning the data into K clusters based on the mean distance of the features.
The mean value of a set of data points in a specific feature that is used as the representative point of the cluster it belongs to in K-Means Clustering.
A measure of the distance between two points in n-dimensional space, calculated as the square root of the sum of the squared differences between corresponding coordinates.
A type of clustering algorithm that builds a hierarchy of clusters by merging or splitting existing clusters, resulting in a tree-like structure of clusters.
A measure of the separation between clusters and the cohesion within clusters, used to evaluate the quality of the clustering results in Hierarchical Clustering.
